name: From Relleu to Villajoyosa – Circular via the Amadorio Reservoir
name_original: Desde Relleu hasta la Villajoyosa – Circular por el Embalse del Amadorio
region_intro: Touring cycling around Relleu is defined by its rugged and diverse natural landscape, characterized by a valley encircled by prominent mountains such as Cabeçó d'Or and Sierra de la Grana. The Amadorio River has sculpted a deep canyon, featuring the Relleu Reservoir, an artificial lake from the 17th century. The terrain includes characteristic dry terraces climbing the slopes, alongside olive and almond groves. Best touring cycling routes around Relleu The most popular touring cycling route is From Relleu to Villajoyosa – Circular via the Amadorio Reservoir, a 24.1 miles (38.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 45 minutes to complete. This route connects Relleu to the coast, passing the Amadorio Reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Port de Tudons – Ascent Puerto de Tudons loop from Sella, a difficult 24.6 miles (39.6 km) path. This route includes the challenging Alto de Teix (El Collao) climb, known for sections with gradients between 12% and 15%. Local touring cyclists also love the Ascent Puerto de Tudons – Port de Tudons loop from Racó del Pas, a 13.4 miles (21.6 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 33 minutes. Touring cycling around Relleu is defined by challenging mountain passes, the Amadorio River valley, and picturesque olive and almond groves. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy routes to demanding ascents. The routes in Relleu are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 130 reviews. More than 4,700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Relleu's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Relleu?
A: Relleu is characterized by its rugged and diverse natural landscape, nestled in a valley surrounded by prominent mountains like Cabeçó d'Or and Sierra de la Grana. You'll encounter challenging mountain passes, such as the Alto de Teix (El Collao) with gradients up to 15%, alongside picturesque olive and almond groves and dry terraces. The Amadorio River has also sculpted a deep canyon, adding to the dramatic topography.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Relleu?
A: While Relleu is known for its challenging mountain passes, there are options for various ability levels. Out of over 100 touring cycling routes in the area, 9 are classified as easy, offering a gentler introduction to the region's beautiful scenery. These routes allow you to enjoy the landscape without the demanding ascents.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning views and pass by significant natural features. You can cycle past the 17th-century Relleu Reservoir, an artificial lake close to the sea, or experience the dramatic Amadorio Canyon. Keep an eye out for views of the majestic Puig Campana, and consider routes that pass highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/619834" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rabosa Pass&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1134855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port de Tudons&lt;/a&gt;. The area is also rich with olive and almond groves.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Relleu?
A: Yes, Relleu offers several circular touring cycling routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952607729/from-relleu-to-villajoyosa-circular-via-the-amadorio-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Relleu to Villajoyosa – Circular via the Amadorio Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which connects Relleu to the coast. Another challenging circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31797684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port de Tudons – Ascent Puerto de Tudons loop from Sella&lt;/a&gt;, which includes the demanding Alto de Teix climb.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Relleu?
A: Relleu is an excellent destination for cycling for much of the year. The region generally boasts good weather conditions, making spring and autumn particularly pleasant for touring cycling, as temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summers can be hot, especially for challenging climbs, while winters are generally mild but can have cooler days.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Relleu?
A: While many routes in Relleu are geared towards experienced cyclists due to the mountainous terrain, there are 9 easy routes available that could be suitable for families looking for a less strenuous ride. These routes typically feature gentler gradients and shorter distances, allowing for a more relaxed cycling experience.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about cycling in Relleu?
A: The touring cycling routes around Relleu are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 130 reviews. Cyclists often praise the stunning scenery, challenging mountain passes, and the generally good road surfaces with minimal traffic, which enhances the overall riding experience.

Q: Where can I find parking for my car and bike in Relleu?
A: Relleu is a small village, and while specific dedicated cycling parking facilities aren't extensively detailed, you can typically find parking within or on the outskirts of the village. It's advisable to look for public parking areas or consult local information upon arrival for the most convenient spots to start your cycling tour.

Q: Can I reach Relleu by public transport with my bike?
A: Public transport options to Relleu are generally limited, reflecting its rural location. While some bus services might connect Relleu to larger towns, carrying bikes on these services can be restricted or require specific arrangements. It's recommended to check with local transport providers for their current policies regarding bicycles before planning your trip.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to eat along the cycling routes or in Relleu?
A: Relleu village itself offers local cafes and restaurants where you can refuel before or after your ride, experiencing local gastronomy, particularly olive oil production. While some longer routes might pass through other small villages with amenities, it's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on more remote sections, as services can be sparse between towns.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites to explore in Relleu?
A: Beyond cycling, Relleu offers cultural and historic attractions. You can explore the ruins of an 11th-century Moorish castle, visit the 17th-century San Jaime Apóstol Church, or see the Hermitage of Sant Albert. The well-preserved town center, with its narrow and steep streets, also provides a charming glimpse into the town's history.

Q: Are there any permits required for cycling in the Relleu area?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for touring cycling on public roads and established trails in the Relleu area. However, if you plan to venture into protected natural areas or private land, it's always best to check for any local regulations or access restrictions that might apply. For the most part, the region's roads are freely accessible for cyclists.
tips: One of the best tours I've ridden. Remains in memory. Magnificent landscape, great little mountain villages and the descent is rapid. It remains a sublime feeling to have moved there in the mountains 🚴🏻‍♂️. Recommended!

If you are planning a tour to the reservoir (Embalse de Amadorio) near Orxeta, you should not hesitate to visit the mountain village of Orxeta, which is a little higher up and off the main road. Lots of small, pretty streets where friendly people live. Worth it.
Very nice beach with a wide promenade. The backdrop is impressive! Postcard ambience! 😉
The reservoir can be circumnavigated very well by bike. The most beautiful view of the lake is from the dam wall.

waypoints.name: Alto de Aitana Scenic Road: Sella to Relleu
